name: Sierra de Montecoche Cycle Loop
name_original: Carril cicloturista Sierra de Montecoche - alrededor del Charco Redondo – circular desde Los Barrios
description: This difficult 31.1-mile touring cycling loop in Los Alcornocales Natural Park offers panoramic views and challenging off-road terrain.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Los Barrios traverse diverse landscapes in the Campo de Gibraltar area of Andalusia, Spain. The region features varied terrain, including Mediterranean vegetation, coastal stretches, and areas within natural parks such as the Parque Natural del Estrecho. Routes often include both paved and unpaved sections, with options for significant elevation gains. This geographical variety provides a range of experiences for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Los Barrios The most popular touring cycling route is Sierra de Montecoche Cycle Loop, a 31.1 miles (50.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 47 minutes to complete. This difficult circular route features a substantial elevation gain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Hoyo de Don Pedro Viewpoint – Rocky path loop from Los Barrios, a moderate 17.9 miles (28.8 km) path. This loop offers varied terrain and includes a viewpoint. Local touring cyclists also love the Ojén Trail – El Cobre Aqueduct loop from Los Barrios, a 50.8 miles (81.8 km) trail leading through diverse landscapes, often completed in about 6 hours 23 minutes. Touring cycling around Los Barrios is defined by diverse Mediterranean scenery, natural park areas, and coastal stretches. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Los Barrios are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Los Barrios's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Los Barrios?
A: Los Barrios offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 25 options to explore. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ranging from moderate to difficult, and traverse diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Los Barrios?
A: The touring cycling routes in Los Barrios feature diverse terrain. You'll encounter a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces, with some segments offering significant elevation gains. The landscape is characterized by Mediterranean vegetation, coastal stretches, and areas within natural parks like the Parque Natural del Estrecho.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes in Los Barrios?
A: Yes, Los Barrios is known for its challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976126958/sierra-de-montecoche-cycle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Montecoche Cycle Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 50.1 km circular route with a substantial 780m elevation gain, requiring very good fitness. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5284965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojén Trail – El Cobre Aqueduct loop from Los Barrios&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 81.8 km with over 1,100m of elevation.

Q: Can I find routes with scenic viewpoints or natural features in Los Barrios?
A: Absolutely. Many routes offer stunning views and pass through areas of natural beauty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31549498" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Pantallas – View of the Rock of Gibraltar loop from Los Barrios&lt;/a&gt; provides iconic vistas of the Rock of Gibraltar. You can also explore natural monuments and caves in the wider region, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/341143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gibraltar Skywalk&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3748096" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;St. Michael's Cave&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any moderate touring cycling routes suitable for a good day out?
A: Yes, there are several moderate routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10283594" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoyo de Don Pedro Viewpoint – Rocky path loop from Los Barrios&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate 28.8 km loop with a 440m elevation gain, offering varied terrain and a rewarding viewpoint.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Los Barrios?
A: The touring cycling routes in Los Barrios are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse Mediterranean scenery, the variety of terrain, and the opportunity to explore natural park areas and coastal stretches.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Los Barrios?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Los Barrios are circular, making them convenient for starting and ending at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976126958/sierra-de-montecoche-cycle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Montecoche Cycle Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10283594" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoyo de Don Pedro Viewpoint – Rocky path loop from Los Barrios&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural parks can I cycle through near Los Barrios?
A: Several touring cycling routes in the region pass through or near significant natural areas. The EuroVelo 8 route, for instance, traverses the Parque Natural del Estrecho, offering opportunities to experience diverse ecosystems and coastal landscapes.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I might encounter on a touring bike ride?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you might encounter historical or geological points of interest. The region is home to natural monuments and caves, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/935992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O'Hara's Battery&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5353638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Europa Point Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, particularly if your route takes you towards Gibraltar.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes starting from Los Barrios?
A: For those seeking longer distances, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5284965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojén Trail – El Cobre Aqueduct loop from Los Barrios&lt;/a&gt; is an extensive 81.8 km ride. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31842755" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Barrios Train Station – San Roque Railway Station loop from Los Barrios&lt;/a&gt;, covering 46.6 km with significant elevation.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Los Barrios?
A: While specific seasonal recommendations are not detailed, the Mediterranean climate of Los Barrios generally makes spring and autumn ideal for cycling, offering milder temperatures and pleasant conditions. Summers can be hot, and winters are typically mild.

Q: Is it possible to see the Rock of Gibraltar from any of the cycling routes?
A: Yes, you can enjoy views of the iconic Rock of Gibraltar on certain routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31549498" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Pantallas – View of the Rock of Gibraltar loop from Los Barrios&lt;/a&gt; is specifically highlighted for offering these spectacular vistas.
tips: Views of the Charco Redondo reservoir (and the A-381 road that crosses it) and peaks of the Sierra del Niño
This curious rock formation attracts a lot of attention and its name is due to the imagination of some who have assimilated it with that shape.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Montecoche Cycle Loop suitable for touring bicycles?
faqs.answer: While listed as a touring bicycle route, the Sierra de Montecoche Cycle Loop is primarily a challenging mountain biking trail. It features significant stretches of unpaved, natural paths and gravel, with some sections being very tough and complicated. A robust gravel bike or mountain bike is highly recommended over a traditional touring bicycle.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycle loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and challenging. You'll encounter a mix of surfaces, including unpaved natural paths, gravel, and some asphalt sections. Be prepared for technical segments that may require dismounting, especially if you're not on a mountain bike.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop offers several notable sights. You'll get a Panoramic view from Montecoche, pass by the scenic Charco Redondo Reservoir, and see the unique Montera del Torero natural monument. The entire route is within the beautiful Parque Natural Los Alcornocales.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Sierra de Montecoche Cycle Loop?
faqs.answer: This loop is considered difficult. It involves significant climbs and descents, making it physically demanding. The challenging off-road conditions and technical sections require a high level of fitness and experience with similar terrain.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Sierra de Montecoche Cycle Loop?
faqs.answer: The loop typically starts from Los Barrios, which is a good place to find parking. Specific parking areas near the trailhead can be found by searching for 'parking Los Barrios' or 'Parque Natural Los Alcornocales access points' in your navigation app.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Montecoche Cycle Loop is located within the Parque Natural Los Alcornocales. Generally, there are no entrance fees for accessing the natural park itself for activities like cycling. However, it's always wise to check the official park regulations for any specific permits required for certain activities or areas, especially for large groups or events.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de Montecoche Cycle Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As the trail is within the Parque Natural Los Alcornocales, dogs are generally permitted. However, they must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Please also ensure you clean up after your dog.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Sierra de Montecoche Cycle Loop?
faqs.answer: Spring (March to May) and Autumn (September to November) are generally the best times. The weather is milder, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making the strenuous climbs more challenging, while winters can bring cooler temperatures and potentially wet, muddy conditions on the unpaved sections.

faqs.question: Does the Sierra de Montecoche Cycle Loop connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers significant portions of the Carril Ciclotourista Sierra de Montecoche and sections of the Andalucía (Province of Cádiz) and Transandalus trails. You'll also find smaller segments of Corredor Verde Dos Bahías and La Teja.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or water stops along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop passes through a natural park, so facilities directly on the trail are limited. It's highly recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks for the entire duration. Los Barrios, the starting point, offers cafes and shops where you can stock up before and after your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on this cycle loop?
faqs.answer: You can expect stunning natural beauty and a privileged environment. The route offers panoramic vistas, especially from Montecoche, and takes you through the heart of the Parque Natural Los Alcornocales. You'll see the Charco Redondo reservoir and the surrounding peaks of the Sierra del Alcornocales, providing a truly immersive outdoor experience.
